page: Amazon Web Services r8a.metal-24xl is a bare-metal, memory-optimized server powered by an AMD EPYC 9R45 processor. It features 96 dedicated physical cores and 96 vCPUs operating at a clock speed of 4.5 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. The hardware configuration includes 768.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory running at 6 GHz, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per core. This server does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, relying instead on network-attached storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 40 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks place this instance in the top tier for both single-core and multi-core CPU execution, as demonstrated by stress-ng and Geekbench evaluations. Memory bandwidth is highly competitive, with cached read speeds reaching 16,807.2 GB/sec and uncached write speeds at 215.78 GB/sec. Database and key-value store performance is notable, with Redis SET operations achieving 235.68 million operations per second and static web serving throughput reaching 117.49 GB/sec. For LLM inference, the server processes prompts efficiently across small, medium, and large models, though text generation for smaller 135M models drops to average tier performance.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the bare-metal architecture maximizes resource density by eliminating hypervisor overhead, offering dedicated access to all 96 physical cores. However, the lack of local NVMe storage requires external storage solutions, which may introduce latency tradeoffs for I/O-heavy workloads. This server is optimized for memory-intensive and high-compute workloads, such as large-scale in-memory databases, enterprise-grade Redis deployments, heavy software compilation, and CPU-based LLM inference tasks.
description: Amazon Web Services r8a.metal-24xl is a bare-metal, memory-optimized server featuring 96 dedicated physical cores powered by an AMD EPYC 9R45 processor running at 4.5 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture, it delivers 768.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 6 GHz, yielding 8.0 GB of RAM per core. The server lacks local storage and dedicated GPUs, utilizing a 40 Gbps baseline network interface. Benchmark results place this instance in the top 10% for single-core and multi-core CPU performance, memory bandwidth, and database operations, with Redis SET operations reaching 235.68 million operations per second. While LLM prompt processing is highly efficient, text generation for smaller models exhibits average performance. This bare-metal configuration is highly suited for memory-intensive applications, large-scale databases, and heavy software compilation, offering high resource density by eliminating virtualization overhead.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r8a.metal-24xl is a bare-metal, memory-optimized server with 96 AMD EPYC cores, 768 GB of DDR5 RAM, and 40 Gbps network bandwidth, built for high-performance database and memory-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r8a.metal-24xl is a bare-metal server featuring 96 AMD EPYC cores and 768 GB DDR5 RAM for memory-intensive workloads.
tagline: A bare-metal, memory-optimized platform featuring ninety-six dedicated AMD EPYC cores and high-speed DDR5 memory for demanding enterprise workloads.

768.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory at 6 GHz
Top-tier CPU and memory bandwidth benchmark performance
No local storage or GPU accelerators included
Optimized for in-memory databases, Redis, and software compilation
